meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Scale Complex Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver professional, production-ready software fast.
content.answer_block: To escape the trap of basic app development, adopt a modular SaaS starter kit architecture. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you can automate boilerplate, enforce strict type safety, and deploy scalable features in hours rather than weeks, effectively doubling your freelance output capacity.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: another client arrives with a vision, and you find yourself reinventing the authentication, database schema, and payment integration wheel for the tenth time. This 'basic app' cycle is a career killer, locking you into low-margin work while your peers ship complex, high-value systems at record speeds.

The secret isn't working harder; it’s building a proprietary SaaS starter kit that acts as your private production line. By combining the rapid architectural blueprinting of Antigravity with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you transform from a manual coder into a high-leverage software architect who delivers production-ready value on day one.

steps.title: Initialize Architecture with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation. Use Antigravity to generate a secure, type-safe backbone that includes RBAC, database ORM, and API middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-grade SaaS starter kit. Include Next.js 14, Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL, NextAuth for multi-provider support, and Stripe integration. Ensure strict TypeScript configuration and modular directory structure.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Stop manually writing SQL files. Use Claude Code to map your domain models directly from your schema definitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate migration scripts for the user profile, subscription tier, and project management entities based on the current Drizzle schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate migrations for all pending schema changes'

steps.title: Implement Enterprise-Grade Auth
steps.description: Secure your starter kit with battle-tested authentication patterns that handle session management and user roles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure NextAuth with JWT rotation and database session storage. Create a custom middleware to protect private routes based on user role-based access control (RBAC).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'secure all routes in /dashboard using the new RBAC middleware'

steps.title: Integrate Payment Lifecycle
steps.description: Connect Stripe webhooks to your internal state to ensure subscription logic is bulletproof and automated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler that listens for subscription.created, subscription.updated, and subscription.deleted, mapping these to the user subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe webhook handler in /api/webhooks/stripe'

steps.title: CI/CD and Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Ensure your starter kit is deployable to Vercel/AWS with a single command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests before deploying to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate github actions pipeline for production deployment'

pro_tips: Inject your 'Core UI Library' into the Antigravity template to ensure every new project carries your unique design signature.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store client-specific requirements so you don't have to re-explain project constraints.
Standardize your error handling middleware across all projects to reduce debugging time during client handovers by 40%.
Keep a 'feature vault' folder in your kit; use Claude Code to selectively pull in pre-built modules like 'CSV Import' or 'PDF Generation' as needed.
Always run a dry-run of your migration scripts via Claude Code before pushing to production to catch potential data loss issues.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is finalized, create a private GitHub repository template. Use this as your 'Golden Image' for all future client projects to ensure 100% consistency and zero setup time.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your tools and start engineering your future. Our community members have reduced their development time by an average of 65% by adopting this exact architectural approach. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
